mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising real auto sales and declining federal funds rates amplifies consumer discretionary spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or positive returns fail to materialize in >60% of co-occurring periods.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing activity and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate profit growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 for the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 250, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 250, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in labor and construction sectors, reinforcing consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment reflects synchronized economic weakness, which directly pressures corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE is followed by positive 60-day returns in >65% of cases during contraction phases, or the signal's predictive power disappears in walk-forward tests.
